nodes.label: Maintain minimum contrast of 4.5:1 for body text and 3:1 for bold headlines ≥18 px. White on #92278F
All images require alt text. The email must communicate indication and safety even with images off —
Minimum live-text size is 10 px (footnotes); body text never below 12 px; tap targets must be ≥44 px
Maintain a logical heading order with exactly one H1 per email.
Underline links; don't rely on color alone to indicate them. Use semantic <table role="presentation"

With fixed section padding tokens, every boundary resolves to one predictable gap: Editorial→Editori
Two hard rules: (1) Never override the vertical padding token — a designer picks Editorial or Dark f
Headlines are set in Title Case (h1.case); all other copy is sentence case (body.case / h2.case).

All caps is also required for callouts (callout.case) and CTAs (cta.button.label.case).

Do not bold: whole body paragraphs, footnotes, references, links (links are underlined, not bolded),
Do not combine bold + italic in email body. Italic (Arial Italic) is only for journal titles in refe
Every SFMC email is built by stacking components in a mandatory sequence: (1) Top Matter — LOCKED, s
Top Matter and End Matter are LOCKED, supplied components — do not build or modify them.
Choose one of the two pre-made template formations shown in the wireframes. Remove any unnecessary s
Elements within a section may be removed if unneeded (e.g., omitting a button or image), but new ele
Both Desktop (600 px) and Mobile (375 px) layouts are provided for every component — SFMC serves the
[INHERITED] The first content section must open cleanly against the bottom edge of the locked Top Ma
Top Matter is supplied pre-built and LOCKED per audience (DTP = logo without BID, patient-friendly s
The email's audience is fixed by the supplied header (DTP vs HCP). All content-section copy and imag
End Matter is supplied pre-built and LOCKED, containing the full FDA-reviewed ISI, Ardelyx address/c
Email / Primary 1 Column (optional) is a single-column primary content block (~600×1138 desktop / ~3
Email / Primary 2 Column (optional) is a two-column layout (~600×1088 desktop / ~375×1538 mobile, co

Use the phrase "IBS-C symptoms" (constipation, belly pain, and/or bloating) — not "your condition" o
Patient-story modules must carry the disclosure "Individual results and experiences may vary. [Name]
Efficacy claims must carry the approved qualifier "*Results may vary. Study results seen in 12- and
Approved DTP CTA labels are short verb phrases in sentence case (not ALL CAPS), 1–3 words — e.g. "Ex

DTP CTAs do not use the HCP library default "Get connected"; that label is HCP-scope only.

Alternate general CTA right-side image, locked to Periwinkle #7B7BED background.
ISI must be live text, never image-based
Do not duplicate ISI fragments, indication statements, or legal lines in content sections
Never repeat the IBSRELA logo lockup inside a content section
Campaign name capitalization in body copy
Use 'IBS-C symptoms' phrasing for patient-facing copy
Teal #0F99BC reserved for LPGA CTA background only
One Wave graphic and one key-strategic-question callout per email; max ~3 CTAs
Only the title/first line may be purple; no other text purple
Never set body copy in Purple or Green

edges.note: The one-H1 heading-order obligation is called out alongside the minimum size/tap-target rules and refines the logical heading-order statement.
Both belong to the baseline accessibility topic group.
Flush stacking with no margins makes edge-theme declaration necessary to avoid seams.
QA verifies theme consistency established by the transition rule.
QA confirms content obligations tied to closed-vocabulary and locked-component rules.
Title-purple exclusivity refines the general title color binding.
Both forbid purple/green in body copy.
Purple restriction interacts with single-color-per-sentence rule.
Both govern the Flip The Script campaign palette usage.
Opacity pairings apply to the same campaign color set as the scene pairings.
In email, gradient text is forbidden; the sparing gradient-headline allowance does not apply — use the split-headline technique instead.
The headline/callout gradient usage references the primary Dark Blue → Purple gradient.
Contrast pairings resolve per the single email-wide theme's panel colors.
Constrains the green accent panel that appears in the fixed background sequence to a single, exclusive use.
Edge-to-edge transitions are how the sequenced background changes are rendered.
Golf/teal gating narrows the general image-locked-to-background pairing rule.
When a populated section sits above, the image-free path overrides the right-side image pairing.
Both driven by the same light/dark background group split within the CTA section.
Approved image backgrounds are all dark-group, forcing white button / green label.
Baseline build technique implements the dual 600/375 width requirement.
Padding values are all multiples of the 8 px base unit.
Edge-to-edge stacking relies on each section owning its own padding.
Grid and gutter values conform to the 8-pt system.
Both define explicit callout styles for specific content devices.
Related callout-style specifications differing by content trigger.
Completeness/no-substitution rule reinforces the approved style and order binding.
Placement/following-content applies alongside the trio's icon style and order within the same section.
CTA section text (and button) follows the global left-alignment rule while any supporting image sits to the right.
Constrains the Heavy weight within the broader hierarchy to a single reserved use.
In email/PPT surfaces, Arial substitutes for the Avenir families named in the hierarchy.
Boundary gaps are the deterministic outcome of the section-owned padding tokens.
Enforcement rules that protect the section-padding-ownership model.
Locked ISI/footer type build is exempt from editorial type-scale defaults.
Campaign FLIP THE SCRIPT headlines override the default headline Title Case.
In body copy the campaign name is title-cased, not the all-caps display treatment.
Display lockup is the styled form of the all-caps campaign headline.
Positive reservation and prohibited targets are two sides of the same bolding policy.
Emphasis controls governing bold and italic apply together in body copy.
Locked-component protection refines the mandatory assembly order.
Element-level removal rule refines the section-level no-add constraint.
Clean-edge seaming depends on the locked bracketing established by the assembly order.
Both are inherited consequences of the LOCKED Top Matter.
Content body typography must match locked ISI body settings referenced in End Matter.
Both govern the patient-facing voice/terminology of the same DTP copy.
Poll response button is a specific styled instance of the general DTP CTA label conventions.
Topic-matching constrains which approved label is chosen per section.
Excludes the HCP default label from the approved DTP label set.
